subject: DevOps
title: Pods: The Smallest Unit
description: A quick win: understand Pods: The Smallest Unit.

hook.text: Kubernetes doesn't run containers directly. It runs pods. A pod wraps one or more containers into a single unit.

buildup.text: Most pods run one container. But sometimes a sidecar container handles logging or proxying alongside the main app.

discovery.text: Containers in a pod share the same network and storage. They communicate over localhost, not over the network.

twist.text: Pods are ephemeral. They're born, they run, they die. Never expect a pod to live forever. Design for replacement.

climax.text: You rarely create pods directly. Controllers like Deployments manage pod lifecycle, scaling, and updates for you.

punchline.text: Pods wrap containers. Deployments manage pods. Think in layers.

quiz.question: Why do containers in the same pod share localhost?
options: They share the same network namespace
Kubernetes copies network packets between them
They run on different nodes but are linked
